plan_metadata.name: Feature Remove App From Devportal And Reprovision

steps.description: Click on the "Declarative Agent" option within the "New Project" dropdown menu to create an agent using instructions and actions.
Click on the "General Teams Agent" option within the dropdown menu titled "Teams Agent or App Using Teams AI Library" at coordinates (474, 210).
Click on the dropdown option "Tab" under the "Teams Capability" menu in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface.
Click the "Default folder /home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects" option in the workspace folder selection dialog within Visual Studio Code, under the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension interface.
Use keyboard shortcut ctrl+shift+~ to open a new terminal in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension panel within Visual Studio Code.
Type text: 'open https://dev.teams.microsoft.com/' into the bash terminal in Visual Studio Code, within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit workspace.
Press the Enter key in the VS Code integrated terminal, executing the command to open https://dev.teams.microsoft.com.
Click the "Use another account" option in the Microsoft account selection dialog on the login.microsoftonline.com sign-in page, below the listed accounts and next to the plus icon.
Click on the "Email or phone" input field in the Microsoft sign-in dialog on the login.microsoftonline.com page within Google Chrome, as indicated by the red crosshair.
Type ${{env:M365_ACCOUNT_NAME}}  into the username or email input field on the Microsoft sign-in page in Google Chrome.
Click the blue "Next" button on the Microsoft login page to proceed after entering the email address (test016@xxbdw.onmicrosoft.com) in Google Chrome.
Click on the "Password" input field in the Microsoft login window within Google Chrome. The red crosshair marks the exact entry point for password input during the authentication process.
Type  ${{secret:M365_ACCOUNT_PASSWORD}} into the password input field on the Microsoft login page for the account ${{M365:M365_ACCOUNT_NAME}} in Google Chrome.
Click the "Sign in" button on the Microsoft login page in Google Chrome, immediately below the password field, to submit the entered password for authentication.
Click the "Yes" button in the Microsoft "Stay signed in?" dialog on the login.microsoftonline.com webpage, indicated by the red crosshair at coordinates (652, 542).
Click the "Apps" menu item in the left sidebar of the Microsoft Teams Developer Portal web interface.
Click the "Search app" input field in the Apps section of the Microsoft Teams Developer Portal web interface to activate search functionality.
Type "${{var:app_name}}" into the search input field in the "Apps" section of the Microsoft Teams Developer Portal web interface, next to the "Import app" button.
Press the Enter key in the search input field on the "Apps" page of Microsoft Teams Developer Portal to execute a search for "vscuse_app_q3p3Qdev".
Click the empty white space below the app list in the Apps tab of the Microsoft Teams Developer Portal web interface.

Press the right arrow key to move focus across columns in the "Apps" list table within the Microsoft Teams Developer Portal, highlighting fields such as "App name", "Version", "App ID", or "Last updated".
Press the right arrow key to horizontally scroll the app list table in the Apps section of the Microsoft Teams Developer Portal web interface, revealing additional columns.
Press the right arrow key to shift focus within the "Apps" tab of the Microsoft Teams Developer Portal, specifically in the app list table containing search results for "sso".
Click the red plus (+) button in the "Action" column to add or perform an action on the listed app within the Microsoft Teams Developer Portal's Apps interface.
Click the "Delete" option (with trash bin icon) in the action menu for an app entry in the Microsoft Teams Developer Portal under the "Apps" section.
Type #text:${{var:app_name}}dev into the confirmation input field within the "Delete App" dialog in the Microsoft Teams Developer Portal web application.
Click the "Confirm" button on the "Delete App" confirmation dialog in the Microsoft Teams Developer Portal to permanently delete the app.
Click the "Close" button (X) at the top-right corner of the Google Chrome window in the Apps: Developer Portal interface.
Click the "Provision" option under the "LIFECYCLE" section in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit sidebar within Visual Studio Code, as indicated by the red crosshair on the left panel.
Click the "Provision" button in the Azure account confirmation dialog within Visual Studio Code, confirming the provisioning of resources using the listed accounts.
Press the F1 key to activate the Command Palette in Visual Studio Code, within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit workspace interface.
Type the text 'notifications:shownotification' into the Visual Studio Code command palette input field at the top of the interface.
Click the "Notifications: ShowNotification" command suggestion in the VS Code command palette to trigger notification functionality within the Microsoft 365 Agents extension interface.
@assertion notification \"provision stage executed successfully\" exist.
